steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage contrasts the temporary nature of spoken sermons with the enduring permanence of printed books.

The decisive phrase is ‘remaining in every man’s possession…conveys its contents for ages to come,’ which directly emphasizes how physical records outlast spoken words.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice A captures the contrast by stating that written words last longer than spoken ones.

The other three either reverse this relationship (B), introduce unsupported ideas (C refers to ‘experts’ not discussed; D focuses on authors, not messages), or contradict the core claim.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
